# Step 9 — Arm × FcγR Interaction Tests {#step9}

Steps 4 and 7 showed the FcγR(→SBA) coupling descriptively stronger in TdaP than TT. This step
turns that into a formal test: whether the FcγR→outcome mapping **differs by maternal arm**, rather
than differing only in the amount pushed through a shared mapping. Two tests per antigen and mode:
a focal **FcγR3b × arm** interaction on `r cfg$outcome_label`, and a likelihood-ratio test of the
whole **FcγR block × arm** (does adding arm-interaction terms to `FcγR2a + FcγR3b` improve fit).
Predictors are standardized within the pooled sample; reference arm is `r cfg$maternal_arms[1]`.

```{r step9-run, results='asis'}
.stage(9, "arm × FcγR interaction tests on the outcome")
if (!RUN_ARM_TESTS) {
  cat("*Step 9 skipped (run_arm_tests = FALSE).*\n\n")
} else {
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    if (!(OUTCOME %in% names(wd)) || !("arm_name" %in% names(wd))) { cat("*Outcome/arm missing.*\n\n"); next }
    rows <- list()
    for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
      r2a <- paste0(A, "_FcgR2a"); r3b <- paste0(A, "_FcgR3b")
      if (!all(c(r2a, r3b) %in% names(wd))) next
      d <- wd %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(OUTCOME, r2a, r3b, "arm_name"))) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
      if (nrow(d) < 40) next
      d[[r2a]] <- as.numeric(scale(d[[r2a]])); d[[r3b]] <- as.numeric(scale(d[[r3b]]))
      d$arm <- factor(d$arm_name, levels = cfg$maternal_arms)
      # focal: FcγR3b × arm
      fA <- lm(stats::as.formula(paste0(OUTCOME, " ~ ", r3b, " * arm")), data = d)
      ci <- summary(fA)$coefficients
      irow <- grep(paste0("^", r3b, ":arm"), rownames(ci))
      # FcγR block × arm: LRT (F-test) of interaction terms
      f_add <- lm(stats::as.formula(paste0(OUTCOME, " ~ (", r2a, " + ", r3b, ") + arm")), data = d)
      f_int <- lm(stats::as.formula(paste0(OUTCOME, " ~ (", r2a, " + ", r3b, ") * arm")), data = d)
      av <- anova(f_add, f_int)
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        antigen = A, n = nrow(d),
        "FcγR3b×arm β" = round(if (length(irow)) ci[irow[1], 1] else NA, 3),
        "FcγR3b×arm p" = signif(if (length(irow)) ci[irow[1], 4] else NA, 3),
        "block×arm F"  = round(av$F[2], 2),
        "block×arm df" = paste0(av$Df[2], ",", av$Res.Df[2]),
        "block×arm p"  = signif(av$`Pr(>F)`[2], 3),
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      .info("step9 [%s] %s interaction done (n=%d)", mode, A, nrow(d))
    }
    tb <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    if (is.null(tb)) { cat("*No antigen had a complete FcγR pair.*\n\n"); next }
    print(knitr::kable(tb, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("Arm-modification of the FcγR\u2192", cfg$outcome_label,
                       " mapping [", mode, "]: focal FcγR3b\u00d7arm coefficient, and F-test of the whole FcγR-block\u00d7arm interaction (reference arm ", cfg$maternal_arms[1], ")."))); cat("\n")
  }
}
```

**How to read this.** A significant `FcγR3b × arm` term means the per-unit FcγR3b→outcome slope
genuinely differs between TdaP and TT — evidence the pathway is *wired* differently, not merely
driven harder in one arm. A significant `block × arm` LRT says the same for the FcγR layer as a
whole. Non-significant interactions with different per-arm strengths (from steps 4/7) would instead
point to different *forcing* through a shared mapping. Read alongside the mixed mode, where the
FcγR/ADCD mediators are quality (quantity-removed): a surviving arm interaction there localises the
divergence to per-antibody engagement rather than to antibody amount.
